concept: Afternoon / Later Part of Day / Time of Continuation
description: The period from noon to evening; the day's mature hours. 'Lorui' names the time of sustained work—energy still present but mellowing, the day moving toward completion.
sound_symbolism: The word flows onward. 'Lo' begins with continuing flow, momentum sustained. 'Ru' rolls through the hours. Finally 'i' arrives at lateness, afternoon deepening.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua lorui' (in the afternoon). The productive hours after midday rest, before evening's gathering.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Afternoon energy is different from morning—working with natural rhythms rather than forcing constant productivity.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Afternoon completed the day's work—the hours when tasks were finished before light faded.
